documentation: offset the intensity accordingly in both linear and log scale

functions.name: xpcs_viewer.module.saxs1d.norm_saxs_data
documentation:
normalize small angle scattering data to enhance the visual difference;
log / linear plot is handled by matplotlib ax objects;
Args:
    Iq: SAXS Intensity, numpy.ndarray
    q: wave transfer;
    plot_norm: [0, 1, 2, 3]
        0: no normalization
        1: q^2
        2: q^4
        3: I / I0
Return:
    Iq: normalized SAXS data
    xlabel: 
    ylabel:
Raise:
    ValueError: if plot_norm not in [0, 1, 2, 3]

classes.name: xpcs_viewer.plothandler.matplot_qt.LineBuilder
documentation:
code copied from
http://chuanshuoge2.blogspot.com/2019/12/matplotlib-mouse-click-event            -draw-line.html
class MplToolbar(MplCanvas):
def __init__(self, **kwargs):
super(MplCanvas, self).__init__(**kwargs)
toolbar = NavigationToolbar2QT(self.fig, self)
layout = QtWidgets.QVBoxLayout()
layout.addWidget(toolbar)
layout.addWidget(self)
widget = QtWidgets.QWidget()
widget.setLayout(layout)
self.setCentralWidget(widget)

functions.name: xpcs_viewer.helper.fitting.fit_xpcs
documentation:
---
param b: bounds
param g2: g2 \[time, ql_dyn\]
param g2_err: \[time, ql_dyn\]
param qd: ql_dyn
param tel: t_el
return: true
---

functions.name: xpcs_viewer.helper.fitting.fit_with_fixed_raw
documentation:
---
param base_func: |-
  the base function used for fitting; it can have multiple
  input variables, some of which can be fixed during the fitting;
param bounds: |-
  tuple with two elements. 1st is the lower bounds and 2nd is
  the upper bounds; if the fit_flag for a variable is False, then the
  upper bound is used as the fixed value;
param fit_flag: tuple of bools, True/False for fit and fixed
param fit_x: the fitting line for x
param p0: |-
  the initial value for the variables; if None is provided, the
  intial value is set as the mean of lower and upper bounds
param sigma: the error for y value
param x: scaler input
param y: scaler output
return: a tuple of (fit_line, fit_val)
---

functions.name: xpcs_viewer.xpcs_file.single_exp_all
documentation:
single exponential fitting for xpcs-multitau analysis
\:param x: delay in seconds
\:param a: contrast
\:param b: tau
\:param c: restriction factor
\:param d: baseline
\:return:

functions.name: xpcs_viewer.xpcs_file.double_exp_all
documentation:
double exponential fitting for xpcs-multitau analysis
Args:
    x: delay in seconds, float or 1d-numpy.ndarray
    a: contrast
    f: fraction for the 1st exp function; the 2nd has (1-f) weight
    b1: tau for 1st exp function
    c1: restriction factor for the 1st exp function
    b2: tau for 2nd exp function
    c2: restriction factor for the 2nd exp function
    d: baseline
Return:
    function value

methods.name: xpcs_viewer.xpcs_file.XpcsFile.get_hdf_info
documentation:
get a text representation of the xpcs file; the entries are organized
in a tree structure;
\:param fstr: list of filter strings, \['string_1', 'string_2', ...\]
\:return: a list strings

methods.name: xpcs_viewer.xpcs_file.XpcsFile.reshape_phi_analysis
documentation:
the saxs1d and stability data are compressed. the values of the empty 
static bins are not saved. this function reshapes the array and fills
the empty bins with nan. nanmean is performed to get the correct
results;

methods.name: xpcs_viewer.xpcs_file.XpcsFile.get_detector_extent
documentation:
get the angular extent on the detector, for saxs2d, qmap/display;
:return:

documentation: get the fitting line for q, within tor

methods.name: xpcs_viewer.xpcs_file.XpcsFile.fit_g2
documentation:
fit the g2 values using single exponential decay function
\:param q_range: a tuple of q lower bound and upper bound
\:param t_range: a tuple of t lower bound and upper bound
\:param bounds: bounds for fitting;
\:param fit_flag: tuple of bools; True to fit and False to float
\:param fit_func: \['single' | 'double'\]: to fit with single exponential

> or double exponential function

```
return

dictionary with the fitting result;
```
XpcsFile is a class that wraps an Xpcs analysis hdf file;
